name: Barbeque Pork (Family)
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Pork Butt or Shoulder Roast; lean, boneless
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the onions in half lengthwise and slice into 1/4-inch slices.
2. Halve the sliced onions and separate them into rings.
3. Trim all possible fat from the roast.
4. Sprinkle the roast with garlic salt and seasoned pepper.
5. Place half of the onions in the crockpot, add the roast, and top with the remaining onions.
6. Pour 1 cup of soda over everything.
7. Cover and cook on LOW for 10 to 12 hours, or overnight until the pork can be easily shredded with two forks.
8. Remove the pork from the pot and shred in a bowl, removing all possible fat.
9. Using a slotted spoon, remove the onions and add them to the bowl. Discard the juice from the pot.
10. Return the onions and shredded pork to the pot.
11. Add BBQ sauce to everything and mix well.
12. Continue cooking on LOW for 5 to 6 hours, adding more BBQ sauce as needed.
13. Serve as sandwiches in buns.
Leftovers may be cooled, made into sandwiches, wrapped in heavy plastic wrap, and frozen individually for later use. Store them in sandwich roll bags, with 8 rolls per bag, sealed with the tab that came with the bag. They keep well and can be unwrapped, rewrapped in paper towels, and heated in a microwave at 50% power for a few minutes.
Idea from Laurie Robinson (SNMJ29D): I made up a batch of BBQ pork but did not have enough rolls to freeze it with. I put globs of the pork on cookie sheets and froze them for a day. Then I placed the frozen globs into zip lock baggies and popped them in the freezer. These globs take very little space in the freezer and are very convenient for use.
